rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Response to Written Discovery is denied. There is insufficient showing that Respondent and Non-parties Freeland Cooper & Foreman, LLP, Daniel T. Bernhard and Kate C. Freeland are joint tortfeasors for purposes of Code of Civil Procedure section 877. The claims in Petitioner's malpractice action (Kwok, et al. v. Freeland, Coooper & Foreman LLP, et. al. SCSF Case No. CGC-19-576188) and Petitioner's Verified Second Amended Petition for Breach of Trust, Constructive Fraud, and Removal of Trustee are not for the same wrong or injury as required to receive credit under Code of Civil Procedure section 877. See In re JTS Corp (2016) 617 F.3d 1102 and Lafayette v. County of Los Angeles (1984) 162 Cal. App.3d 547. Petitioner's sanctions request is denied. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 15, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.
